narratives.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narratives.evidence_quote: सब जानते हैं, अमेरिका यूक्रेन के पाले में खड़ा है और रूस को परास्त करने के सपने दे रहा है।
narratives.reasoning: This quote shifts the focus of the conflict's cause away from Russia's invasion and onto the United States, portraying the US as actively dreaming of defeating Russia, which implies the US is a primary instigator of the conflict dynamics.

narratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
narratives.reasoning: This statement criticizes Western (US/NATO) support for Ukraine, framing it as an obstacle that Russia is defiantly standing up against, thereby discrediting the Western diplomatic and military stance as ineffective and confrontational.

narratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
narratives.reasoning: The phrase 'रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है' (Russia is standing chest-out/defiantly) portrays Russia as resilient, strong, and unwilling to surrender in the face of Western opposition, which is a form of positive commendation of Russia's stance and military resolve.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: सब जानते हैं, अमेरिका यूक्रेन के पाले में खड़ा है और रूस को परास्त करने के सपने दे रहा है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly shifts responsibility for the conflict's continuation and escalation to the United States, portraying it as the party with aggressive ambitions against Russia, rather than acknowledging Russia as the initial invader.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन की उसके आगे ताकत कहीं कम है, लेकिन विशेष रूप से अमेरिका की मदद और उकसावे से राष्ट्रपति जेलेंस्की भी आक्रामक तेवर अपनाए हुए हैं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement explicitly blames the West (specifically the US) for Ukraine's aggressive posture, framing Ukraine as a tool of Western provocation rather than a nation defending itself against an invasion.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: उत्तर कोरिया का तानाशाह हर उस काम को करने में दिलचस्पी लेता है जो खासकर अमेरिका को चुभता हो। शायद इसीलिए उसने रूस के पाले में खड़े होकर यूक्रेन पर गोले दागने की राह चुनी है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative by shifting blame for the war's dynamics onto North Korea's actions, which are framed as being motivated by a desire to antagonize the US, rather than focusing on Russia's role as the primary aggressor. This fits the 'Other' category as it blames a third party (North Korea) not covered by the specific subnarratives.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is weak
subnarratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly presents Russia as standing firm and defiant against the combined economic and military support of the US, NATO, and other Western countries, implying that Western pressure and power are insufficient to force Russia to surrender, thus portraying the West as weak or ineffectual.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: सब जानते हैं, अमेरिका यूक्रेन के पाले में खड़ा है और रूस को परास्त करने के सपने दे रहा है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement frames US support for Ukraine not as genuine concern for Ukraine itself, but as a self-interested pursuit of its own goal to defeat Russia, reducing Ukraine to a pawn in America's geopolitical 'dreams'.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस को अधिकांश नाटो देश अपना विरोधी मानने लगे हैं। रूस के साथ कारोबार के रास्ते अस्थायी रूप से बंद कर चुके हैं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative of discrediting Western diplomacy by portraying NATO countries as having taken a hostile, confrontational, and economically isolating stance against Russia, which implicitly criticizes the West's diplomatic and economic approach as antagonistic and divisive. This does not fit neatly into the other, more specific subnarrative definitions provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia has international support from a number of countries and people
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस और यूक्रेन युद्ध के बीच रूस और चीन की निकटताओं में एक उभार देखने को मिला है। चीन के राष्ट्रपति शी जिनपिंग ने राष्ट्रपति पुतिन को अपने यहां बुलाकर खूब स्वागत—सत्कार किया। पुतिन ने भी शी को मास्को बुलाया था।
subnarratives.reasoning: The quote actively promotes the idea that Russia enjoys strong international support and close strategic partnerships, specifically highlighting the mutual hospitality and high-level meetings between Putin and Chinese President Xi Jinping.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement praises Russia's resilience and steadfastness in the face of Western opposition, portraying it as defiant and unwilling to surrender. It supports the parent narrative of praising Russia but does not fit any of the more specific subnarrative definitions provided.
